methods.description: Construction of analytical potential energy surfaces (PESs) for triatomic systems using ab initio energies obtained from CCSD(T) electronic structure calculations.

steps.description: Analytical PESs were constructed using ab initio energies obtained using CCSD(T) electronic structure calculations.
steps.searchable_summary: perform CCSD(T) electronic structure calculations to obtain ab initio energies

steps.description: For the construction of PESs for the triatomic system ABC, ab initio energies were computed along the grids defined in internal coordinates (r_AB, r_BC, and θ).
steps.searchable_summary: compute ab initio energies along defined grids in internal coordinates

steps.description: To represent the 2B interaction energies, analytical PESs for all the possible diatomic fragments were obtained using either cubic spline interpolation or nonlinear curve fitting methods.
steps.searchable_summary: use cubic spline interpolation or nonlinear curve fitting to obtain diatomic fragment PESs

steps.description: The Levenberg-Marquardt nonlinear optimization algorithm was used to determine the linear and nonlinear parameters in Equation (3).
steps.searchable_summary: apply Levenberg-Marquardt algorithm for parameter optimization

methods.description: Solving the time-dependent Schrödinger equation using wave packets to study quantum mechanical systems.

steps.description: An initial WP (either complex or real) prepared in the reactant asymptotic region is propagated using either the split operator or Chebyshev real WP propagation methods.
steps.searchable_summary: propagate initial wave packet using split operator or Chebyshev methods

steps.description: The action of radial kinetic energy operators are evaluated using the fast Fourier transformation technique.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate radial kinetic energy using FFT

steps.description: The action of the angular momentum operator is evaluated in associated Legendre polynomial basis.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate angular momentum operator in Legendre polynomial basis

steps.description: After sufficient propagation, the energy dependent total reaction probabilities are calculated by summing the total flux passing through a fixed surface located at a large distance in the product channel.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate total reaction probabilities from wave packet flux

methods.description: Using the ABC program to solve the time-independent Schrödinger equation for reactive scattering studies.

steps.description: The time-independent Schrödinger equation is solved using the CC approach in hyperspherical coordinates.
steps.searchable_summary: solve time-independent Schrödinger equation using CC approach

steps.description: The CC hyper-radial equations are solved by using a constant reference potential log derivative method between ρ_max to ρ_min.
steps.searchable_summary: solve CC hyper-radial equations using log derivative method

steps.description: The S-matrix elements for a particular J with diatomic parity (p) and triatomic parity (P) for all the channels are calculated from the final log derivative matrix by applying scattering boundary conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate S-matrix elements using scattering boundary conditions

methods.description: Application of statistical techniques to study reactions proceeding via a complex-forming mechanism using the SQM approach.

steps.description: Capture probabilities are obtained by solving a set of CC equations for each arrangement.
steps.searchable_summary: solve CC equations to obtain capture probabilities

steps.description: The interaction potential matrix is expressed in terms of diatomic vibrational wavefunctions, vector-coupling coefficients, and spherical harmonics.
steps.searchable_summary: express interaction potential matrix using wavefunctions and harmonics

steps.description: The state-to-state probabilities are employed for the calculation of the corresponding integral cross sections (ICS).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate integral cross sections from state-to-state probabilities

steps.description: The ICS is employed for the calculation of the rate constants.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate rate constants from integral cross sections
